title: Using the Autoclave
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4mjb5","text":"Instructions for using the autoclaves in the dishwashing and autoclave facility at UCI, floor 3. Instructions are up to date as of 2025/01/13.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ikp","text":"If you anticipate that the autoclave bucket will be heavy, or you have too many things to fit in the bucket, use the cart for transport.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"990un","text":"Make sure caps are loosened on bottles containing liquid!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare your media to specification, but leave at least 100 mL headroom. This is so the media doesn't boil and spill out of the bottle during the autoclaving process.
For example, if you are preparing media in the 1 L glass bottles, you can only make 900 mL of media so that you retain 100 mL of space at the top of the bottle.
Ensure that your solution is well mixed and there are no clumps of undissovled media powder.
Screw the lid LOOSELY on the bottle. The idea is to allow steam to escape during the autoclaving process such that the bottle won't explode.
Use a piece of tinfoil to cover the cap and next of the bottle and put a piece of thermosensitive (autoclave) tape on the tinfoil.
Place your media bottles in an autoclavable secondary container and take your materials to the autoclaving and dishwashing facility on the 3rd floor of Steinhaus Hall

For most liquid media, we use the Liquid15 cycle which is preprogrammed into the autoclaves. To select an autoclave cycle: Select Cycle -> navigate the list up or down until you find "Liquid 15" -> Select Cycle -> Next -> Start Cycle -> Start Cycle (you have to press select cycle twice)
Wait approximately 1 minute for the autoclave to start. You should hear a click/whirr and see the PSI gauge jump from 0 to 10.

Choose your favorite autoclave. If none are open but you can see that cycles are completed, borrow the thermal gloves from the back wire rack in the facility, carefully remove any material in the autoclave, and place it on the black countertops closest to the autoclaves. In addition to wearing appropriate PPE, maintain some distance whenever opening the autoclaves, the heat rises/ rushes out and will melt your mascara if standing too close. You are essentially opening an incredibly hot oven.
FINAL CHECK! That bottle cap BETTER be loose. Also give your media one last swirl to mix if media has sedimented out on the bottom. This extra swirl almost always needed for ChromoSelect media.
Set your phone timer for 01:00:00 to return to pick up your materials. Bring the orange thermal gloves from the lab. The bin will be hot!
Gather your clean, relatively dry materials.
If you are just autoclaving liquid waste, still ensure that there is appropriate headroom but usually waste is much smaller in volume.
Place your media bottles in an autoclavable secondary container and take your materials to the autoclaving and dishwashing facility on the 3rd floor of Steinhaus Hall

Choose your favorite autoclave. If none are open but you can see that cycles are completed, borrow the thermal gloves from the back wire rack in the facility, carefully remove any material in the autoclave, and place it on the black countertops closest to the autoclaves. In addition to wearing appropriate PPE, maintain some distance whenever opening the autoclaves, the heat rises/ rushes out and will melt your mascara if standing too close. You are essentially opening an incredibly hot oven.

We use the "Prevac" or "Instruments10" cycle which is preprogrammed into the autoclaves. To select an autoclave cycle: Select Cycle -> navigate the list up or down until you find "Prevac" -> Select Cycle -> Next -> Start Cycle -> Start Cycle (you have to press select cycle twice)
Wait approximately 1 minute for the autoclave to start. You should hear a click/whirr and see the PSI gauge jump from 0 to 10.
Set your phone timer for 01:00:00 to return to pick up your materials. Bring the orange thermal gloves from the lab. The bin will be hot!
Use a piece of tinfoil to cover the cap and next of the bottle and put a piece of thermosensitive (autoclave) tape on the tinfoil.
If you are autoclaving things without a cap, just place a piece of tinfoil that fully covers the opening. Ex: flasks, beakers, graduated cylinders.
Add water to the secondary container such that it is 2 centimeters deep or so.
Sometimes there is condensation or excess moisture inside the bottle. Loosen the cap and place the bottle into an incubator at around 50-60ºC until all the moisture has evaporated.
creator: Jaycee Fahrner
